items.rationale: Sees ZK as a foundational, accelerating field unlocking private airdrops, private document/data workflows, and new ways to control protocols. Repeatedly publishes and ships ZK tooling.

citations.quote: I'm excited to share our new zero knowledge cryptography canon.
research has accelerated radically as a result of web3.
@samrags_ brought us private airdrops on Ethereum. Compose zkdrops with a few other privacy systems

items.rationale: Believes building on centralized platforms gets builders 'rugged'; sees neutral, sufficiently-decentralized social protocols as the long-awaited alternative, and tracks a growing client/tool ecosystem.

citations.quote: Farcaster is the neutral platform we wanted all along.
The Farcaster ecosystem is growing, with already a dozen interfaces and clients, and many tools

items.rationale: Views L2 rollups as the path to scaling Ethereum without compromising security; personally ships an OP Stack client (Magi) and champions client diversity for resilience.

citations.quote: Magi is a new OP Stack client for the Optimism ecosystem. Written in Rust... client diversity = robust decentralization
It certainly seems to be exploring L2.
Anybody interested in scaling Ethereum should take a look

items.rationale: Wants to unbundle login and replace username/password auth with wallet keypairs, giving people control of their data; contrasts this with state-controlled single-provider identity.
items.age_note: Core thesis stated 2022-04 to 2022-07; reinforced indirectly via 2023-04-29 critique of centralized state identity.

citations.quote: With DIDs, we can use the public keys generated by crypto wallets... potentially replacing username/password authentication and giving people complete control of their data.
Spruce is building the full stack of open source tools and interop standards needed to unbundle the login

items.rationale: Personally leads engineering output: symbolic testing, light clients, trusted-setup ceremonies. Frames open-source tooling and multiple clients as making Ethereum safer and the ecosystem more robust.

citations.quote: Halmos, a symbolic testing tool for Ethereum smart contracts. With formal methods... we can make Ethereum safer.
more clients... more able to endure bugs and other challenges, but also more flexible and evolvable.

items.rationale: Strong personal conviction that good design starts with a protocol goal, with tokens as a tool for alignment, growth, risk distribution and decentralization — not the goal itself.

citations.quote: A protocol needs a clear goal. Launching a token isn't a goal. Tokens are an incredible tool for growing a protocol, aligning stakeholders
If tokens are the native asset class of information networks, they should provide the broad range of rights and powers

items.rationale: Excited by recent designs bringing privacy to the account/app layer — private voting via timelock puzzles, account-level privacy — as a needed primitive for crypto apps.

citations.quote: Cicada is the cleanest app layer private voting I've seen
Awesome to see a novel approach to privacy at the account/app layer!

items.rationale: Sees the combination of AI ('how machines think') and crypto ('how machines own') enabling self-funding autonomous programs — e.g. a '0-person unicorn' running on a crypto network.

citations.quote: AI = how machines can think; crypto = how machines can own; AI + crypto = how machines can think on their own
the first 0-person unicorn? An autonomous program on a crypto network that can fund and grow itself.

items.rationale: Expects on-chain games to fill the programmer-gamer niche and eventually match deep emergent games; engages closely with the games team and on-chain game projects.

citations.quote: I believe fully on-chain games will fill a similar niche in coming years.
Every game can be as rich and deep as Dwarf Fortress.

items.rationale: Frames NFTs as programmable objects with mutable, interactive state (on-chain games, replicating art) rather than static JPEGs — a building block for richer internet objects.
items.age_note: Stated in a burst in March 2022; not meaningfully restated through mid-2023, so weight is lower.

citations.quote: NFTs are programmable... they can contain complex, mutable state to enable interactivity with people, programs, and other NFTs.

items.rationale: Considers Apple's fees and native-app restrictions a major, underrated drag on innovation and the chief reason crypto mobile UX is poor.

citations.quote: Apple will cripple you, block you, or take your lunch if you try to offer a native experience.
The effects of Apple's 30% fee on innovation are radically underestimated

items.rationale: Recurring thesis that building on closed platforms is unsafe — APIs change, mods/users/3rd-party clients get undermined — motivating the move to neutral, open protocols.

citations.quote: you'll get cut off, replaced by them, blocked from monetizing. Rugged.
Reddit undermines its mods, users, and 3rd party clients at every turn... These API changes are killing my project.

items.rationale: Sharply critical of CEX transparency theater post-FTX, dissecting how Binance's proof-of-reserves was unverifiable and exploitable.
items.age_note: Concentrated in Nov 2022 around the FTX collapse; not restated since, but tied to a structural distrust of centralized custody.

citations.quote: Binance's system is useless and easily exploited by them.
Negative balances are a known fatal flaw of the 'proof of reserves' method Binance shared

items.rationale: Views pie-chart 'tokenomics experts' and allocation-first whitepapers as worthless — a symptom of projects treating a token launch as the goal rather than designing a protocol.

citations.quote: 'Tokenomics experts' who simply 'work' with you to determine 'token allocations' are a complete waste of time.
I've seen 'white papers' where this is the entire paper... It's total nonsense.

items.rationale: Frustrated that mainstream coverage of crypto is inaccurate and that search results are bad enough to deter newcomers — a discovery/perception headwind for the space.
items.age_note: Stated late 2022; not restated by mid-2023.

citations.quote: Imagine explaining to a normal person how outrageous this coverage has been.
Google search results for crypto are bad enough to deter any reasonable person.

items.rationale: Implicitly concerned about regulation; argues well-designed protocol rules that protect users can reduce the need for it — positioning regulation as a force to design around.

citations.quote: If those rules are designed properly to protect users, we can decrease the need for regulation.

items.rationale: Treats Ethereum as the clear leader by developer mindshare and the base layer his work scales/secures; celebrated the Merge and contributes to its scaling (KZG ceremony, light clients, L2 clients).

citations.quote: @ethereum is the clear leader. Its overwhelming mindshare among web3 devs
Congratulations and well done to the ETH 2 researchers, client teams, core devs

items.rationale: Not a price call — a design critique: their essential role as the risk token underwriting the protocol is rhetorically obscured by 'governance' framing, which he sees as a mistake.
items.age_note: Single observation from July 2022; not restated.

citations.quote: the role of MKR and AAVE/COMP as the risk token that underwrites the protocol often seems to take a back seat rhetorically to 'governance'

risk_regime.rationale: Persistently structurally bullish builder, not a trader. Maintains crypto is extremely early ('around 1995'), that underlying tech advances even in 'crypto winter,' and that price-innovation cycles produce consistent long-term growth. His optimism is about building/technology adoption rather than tactical price timing; concerns are about platform incumbents and bad design, not the thesis itself.

notes: Lazzarin is an engineer/protocol-design lead, so his 'views' are overwhelmingly structural theses about technology and design, not tactical token trades — hence few explicit token calls and a long, durable list of sector convictions. Most recent (2023 H1) signal concentrates on shipping ZK tooling, L2/OP Stack clients, formal verification, and app-layer privacy, which I weighted highest. Recurring throughline across the whole corpus: open/neutral protocols beat closed platforms (App Store, web2 social, CEXs) because 'network design has economic consequences.' Late-2022 concerns (Binance PoR, crypto media/search) are FTX-era and not restated by mid-2023, so weighted lower. No statements indicate tactical risk-off positioning; the 'concerns' are obstacles to a thesis he remains committed to.
